response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong seniority with 20 years of software engineering experience across distributed systems and performance optimization, he lacks the fundamental domain expertise required for this role. The position demands hands-on experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, and deep-learning workload optimization—areas entirely absent from his resume. His background is exclusively in software development (web, mobile, backend) rather than hardware design or AI accelerator optimization, making him a poor fit for a principal-level hardware architecture role.
strengths: Extensive seniority with 20+ years of software development and multiple staff-level positions demonstrating leadership capability
Demonstrated performance optimization expertise through low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby, including async I/O, caching, and high-performance database design
Proven adaptability across diverse tech stacks and domains, with experience in microservices, distributed systems, and infrastructure design
concerns: Complete absence of SoC, IP modeling, or hardware design experience—core responsibilities for this principal role
No background in AI/ML workload optimization, deep neural networks, or GPU architecture analysis despite being a required skill
Educational credentials (two bachelor's degrees) fall below the master's/PhD preferred for principal-level work in specialized hardware domains
